title: JournalRecord
description:
003 §"Journal contract" — one record appended by a system-extension writer to its own single-writer segment file.

The framing around this body (length prefix, per-record CRC, and the framing-level `schema_version`) belongs to the segment file layer and is **not** part of this JSON body; [`JournalRecord::SCHEMA_VERSION`] is the value the framing carries for records of this shape.

The op vocabulary is **sealed at rest** (003 r9, payload sealing): `payload_sealed` is the AES-256-GCM ciphertext of the serialized [`JournalOpKind`] document, under `key = HKDF-SHA256(transformed, info = "vaultkern.journal.v1")`, with a fresh random 12-byte nonce per record and `AAD = op_id ‖ vault_ref_id`. The journal is the only on-disk location outside the vault that can hold secrets (passkey private keys); container same-signature isolation argues write integrity, not confidentiality.

base_fingerprint.description: Fingerprint (lowercase hex SHA-256) of the cached vault the extension saw when it appended this record. **Diagnostic only**; replay does not require a match.

created_at.description: Append time, seconds since the Unix epoch.

nonce.description: The record's fresh random 12-byte GCM nonce, encoded as **standard base64 (RFC 4648 §4, with padding)** — same pinned encoding as `payload_sealed`.

op_id.description: UUIDv7 string — the idempotency identity of the mutation. Replay dedup and the applied set key off this value alone; it is also the first component of the sealing AAD.

payload_sealed.description: AES-256-GCM ciphertext (including the GCM tag) of the serialized [`JournalOpKind`] document, encoded as **standard base64 (RFC 4648 §4, with padding)** — the encoding is part of the frozen contract.

seq.description: Monotonic within one writer's segment (single writer per segment ⇒ no allocation contention).

vault_ref_id.description: Target vault, **plaintext**: routing + pre-replay validation. Lives only here (payloads do not duplicate it) and is bound into the sealing AAD, so it cannot be swapped without failing the GCM tag.
